title: scenario_fire_children_trapped
description: Residential fire with children reported trapped on upper floor

timeline.title: Dispatch
timeline.description: 911 call — children reported trapped on second floor of burning home.

timeline.title: En Route
timeline.description: Smoke visible from distance. Multiple calls confirm children at window.

timeline.title: On Scene
timeline.description: Two-story home, fire venting from first floor rear. Two young children visible at second-floor window.

timeline.title: Ladder Raised
timeline.description: Ground ladder raised to second-floor window. Children yelling for help.

timeline.title: Rescue
timeline.description: First child handed down ladder. Second child climbs with firefighter assist.

timeline.title: Fire Controlled
timeline.description: Both children evacuated. Attack line knocked first-floor fire.

node-1.title: Rescue vs Attack Priority
node-1.situation: You arrive to two children visible at a second-floor window. Fire is venting from the rear first floor. The staircase inside may be compromised. You have a truck and engine company.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Assign the truck to raise a ground ladder to the window immediately for rescue while engine advances attack line to protect the staircase from inside.

options.label: B
options.text: Knock the first-floor fire first so the staircase remains safe for the children to self-evacuate.

options.label: C
options.text: Order the children to stay in place and shelter in room until fire is knocked.

node-2.title: Ladder Rescue Execution
node-2.situation: The ladder is against the second-floor window. The older child (approx. 8) is hesitant. The younger child (approx. 4) cannot climb alone. Smoke is increasing at the window.
node-2.question: How do you execute the rescue?

options.label: A
options.text: Firefighter climbs to window, assists smaller child first with a one-arm carry down the ladder, then guides the older child down with a firefighter above for security.

options.label: B
options.text: Tell both children to climb down together while firefighter stabilizes the ladder from the bottom.

options.label: C
options.text: Have the older child toss the smaller child down to crew members below.

node-3.title: Interior Search Confirmation
node-3.situation: Both children are safely evacuated. Parents are unaccounted for. The attack line has knocked the first-floor fire. The staircase is now accessible with moderate smoke.
node-3.question: What is your next action?

options.label: A
options.text: Send a search crew up the stairs with charged line to conduct a primary search for any remaining occupants while maintaining crew accountability.

options.label: B
options.text: Declare the scene clear since the children said no one else was home.

options.label: C
options.text: Begin overhaul of first floor before searching upper floors.

node-4.title: Scenario Complete
node-4.situation: Primary search of the second floor found no additional victims. The parents were confirmed to have self-evacuated before your arrival. Both children were evaluated by EMS — minor smoke inhalation, no injuries. The fire was confined to the rear first floor. Well done — immediate ladder rescue, proper carry technique for the younger child, and confirmatory search were all executed correctly.
node-4.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
